name: Diabetic Mocha Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream together margarine and sugar until light and fluffy.
2. Add vanilla and sugar substitute to the creamed mixture and mix lightly.
3. Stir flour, cocoa, coffee, and baking powder to blend.
4. Add this dry mixture to the creamed mixture and mix at medium speed 1-2 minutes or until blended.
5. Add raisins and nuts to the dough and mix lightly.
6. Add egg whites to the dough and mix until they are absorbed into the dough.
7. Drop dough by tablespoonsful onto a cookie sheet lined with aluminum foil or sprayed with PAM.
8. Using fingers dipped in cold water, shape dough into round cookies about 1/4 inch thick.
9. Spread the dough carefully as the finished cookie will be shaped like the dough after baking.
10. Bake at 375°F for about 12 minutes, or until the dough springs back when touched.
11. Transfer from the hot cookie sheet onto a wire rack and cool to room temperature.
12. Serve one cookie per serving.
